endif;
if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)))
elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)))
elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
D0.f16 = S0.f16
elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
D0.f16 = S1.f16
elsif ((S0.f16 < S1.f16) || ((abs(S0.f16) == 16'0.0) && (abs(S1.f16) == 16'0.0) && sign(S0.f16) &&
!sign(S1.f16))) then
// NOTE: -0<+0 is TRUE in this comparison
D0.f16 = S0.f16
else
D0.f16 = S1.f16
endif

s_maximum_f16.description:
Select the IEEE maximum() of two half-precision float inputs and store the result into a scalar register.
A signaling NaN in either argument is propagated to the result.
s_maximum_f16.operation:
if (isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) || isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f16))) then
TRAPSTS.INVALID = 1
endif;
if isSignalNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)))
elsif isSignalNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
D0.f16 = 16'F(cvtToQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)))
elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S0.f16)) then
D0.f16 = S0.f16
elsif isQuietNAN(64'F(S1.f16)) then
D0.f16 = S1.f16
elsif ((S0.f16 > S1.f16) || ((abs(S0.f16) == 16'0.0) && (abs(S1.f16) == 16'0.0) && !sign(S0.f16) &&
sign(S1.f16))) then
// NOTE: +0>-0 is TRUE in this comparison
D0.f16 = S0.f16
else
D0.f16 = S1.f16
endif

s_add_nc_u64.description: Add two unsigned 64-bit integer inputs and store the result into a scalar register.

s_sub_nc_u64.description: Subtract the second unsigned 64-bit integer input from the first input and store the result into a scalar register.

s_mul_u64.description: Multiply two unsigned 64-bit integer inputs and store the result into a scalar register.
s_mul_u64.notes:
This instruction takes 5 cycles to execute but due to SALU pipeline stalling it is sufficient to use S_DELAY_ALU
INSTID_SALU_CYCLE_1 to address performance of back-to-back "S_MUL_U64; SALU" instructions. For the
back-to-back "S_MUL_64; VALU" case use S_NOP to insert additional delay if needed.
Instructions in this format may not use a 32-bit literal constant that occurs immediately after the instruction.

s_movk_i32.description: Sign extend a literal 16-bit constant and store the result into a scalar register.

s_version.description:
Do nothing. This opcode is used to specify the microcode version for tools that interpret shader microcode.
Argument is ignored by hardware. This opcode is not designed for inserting wait states as the next instruction
may issue in the same cycle. Do not use this opcode to resolve wait state hazards, use S_NOP instead.
This opcode may also be used to validate microcode is running with the correct compatibility settings in
drivers and functional models that support multiple generations. We strongly encourage this opcode be
included at the top of every shader block to simplify debug and catch configuration errors.
This opcode must appear in the first 16 bytes of a block of shader code in order to be recognized by external
tools and functional models. Avoid placing opcodes > 32 bits or encodings that are not available in all versions
of the microcode before the S_VERSION opcode. If this opcode is absent then tools are allowed to make an
educated guess of the microcode version using cues from the environment; the guess may be incorrect and
lead to an invalid decode. It is highly recommended that this be the first opcode of a shader block except for
trap handlers, where it should be the second opcode (allowing the first opcode to be a 32-bit branch to
accommodate context switch).
SIMM16[7:0] specifies the microcode version.
SIMM16[15:8] must be set to zero.
s_version.operation:
nop();
// Do nothing - for use by tools only

s_cmovk_i32.description: Move the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant into a scalar register iff SCC is nonzero.

s_addk_co_i32.description:
Add a scalar input and the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant, store the result into a scalar register and
store the carry-out bit into SCC.

s_mulk_i32.description:
Multiply a scalar input with the sign extension of a literal 16-bit constant and store the result into a scalar
register.

s_getreg_b32.description:
Read some or all of a hardware register into the LSBs of destination.
The SIMM16 argument is encoded as follows:
ID = SIMM16[5:0]

s_setreg_b32.description:
Write some or all of the LSBs of source argument into a hardware register.
The SIMM16 argument is encoded as follows:
ID = SIMM16[5:0]

s_setreg_imm32_b32.description:
Write some or all of the LSBs of a 32-bit literal constant into a hardware register; this instruction requires a 32-
bit literal constant.
The SIMM16 argument is encoded as follows:
ID = SIMM16[5:0]

s_call_b64.description:
Store the address of the next instruction to a scalar register and then jump to a constant offset relative to the
current PC.
The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction. The byte address of
the instruction immediately following this instruction is saved to the destination.
s_call_b64.notes:
This implements a short subroutine call where the return address (the next instruction after the S_CALL_B64)
is saved to D. Long calls should consider S_SWAPPC_B64 instead.
This instruction must be 4 bytes.
Instructions in this format may use a 32-bit literal constant that occurs immediately after the instruction.

s_mov_b32.description: Move scalar input into a scalar register.

s_mov_b64.description: Move scalar input into a scalar register.

s_cmov_b32.description: Move scalar input into a scalar register iff SCC is nonzero.
s_cmov_b32.operation:
if SCC then
D0.b32 = S0.b32
endif

s_cmov_b64.description: Move scalar input into a scalar register iff SCC is nonzero.
s_cmov_b64.operation:
if SCC then
D0.b64 = S0.b64
endif

s_brev_b32.description: Reverse the order of bits in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register.

s_brev_b64.description: Reverse the order of bits in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar register.

s_ctz_i32_b32.description:
Count the number of trailing "0" bits before the first "1" in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar
register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits in the input.
s_ctz_i32_b32.notes:
Functional examples:
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0xaaaaaaaa) => 1
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0x55555555) => 0
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0xffffffff) => 0
S_CTZ_I32_B32(0x00010000) => 16
Compare with V_CTZ_I32_B32, which performs the equivalent operation in the vector ALU.

s_ctz_i32_b64.description:
Count the number of trailing "0" bits before the first "1" in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar
register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits in the input.

s_clz_i32_u32.description:
Count the number of leading "0" bits before the first "1" in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar
register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits.
s_clz_i32_u32.notes:
Functional examples:
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0x0000cccc) => 16
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0xffff3333) => 0
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0x7fffffff) => 1
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0x80000000) => 0
S_CLZ_I32_U32(0xffffffff) => 0
Compare with V_CLZ_I32_U32, which performs the equivalent operation in the vector ALU.

s_clz_i32_u64.description:
Count the number of leading "0" bits before the first "1" in a scalar input and store the result into a scalar
register. Store -1 if there are no "1" bits.

s_cls_i32.description:
Count the number of leading bits that are the same as the sign bit of a scalar input and store the result into a
scalar register. Store -1 if all input bits are the same.
s_cls_i32.notes:
Functional examples:
S_CLS_I32(0x00000000) => 0xffffffff
S_CLS_I32(0x0000cccc) => 16
S_CLS_I32(0xffff3333) => 16
S_CLS_I32(0x7fffffff) => 1
S_CLS_I32(0x80000000) => 1
S_CLS_I32(0xffffffff) => 0xffffffff
Compare with V_CLS_I32, which performs the equivalent operation in the vector ALU.

s_cls_i32_i64.description:
Count the number of leading bits that are the same as the sign bit of a scalar input and store the result into a
scalar register. Store -1 if all input bits are the same.

s_sext_i32_i8.description: Sign extend a signed 8 bit scalar input to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar register.

s_sext_i32_i16.description: Sign extend a signed 16 bit scalar input to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar register.

s_bitset0_b32.description: Given a bit offset in a scalar input, set the indicated bit in the destination scalar register to 0.

s_bitset0_b64.description: Given a bit offset in a scalar input, set the indicated bit in the destination scalar register to 0.

s_bitset1_b32.description: Given a bit offset in a scalar input, set the indicated bit in the destination scalar register to 1.

s_bitset1_b64.description: Given a bit offset in a scalar input, set the indicated bit in the destination scalar register to 1.

s_bitreplicate_b64_b32.description:
Substitute each bit of a 32 bit scalar input with two instances of itself and store the result into a 64 bit scalar
register.
s_bitreplicate_b64_b32.notes:
This opcode can be used to convert a quad mask into a pixel mask; given quad mask in s0, the following
sequence produces a pixel mask in s2:
s_bitreplicate_b64 s2, s0
s_bitreplicate_b64 s2, s2
To perform the inverse operation see S_QUADMASK_B64.

s_abs_i32.description:
Compute the absolute value of a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is
nonzero.
s_abs_i32.notes:
Functional examples:
S_ABS_I32(0x00000001) => 0x00000001
S_ABS_I32(0x7fffffff) => 0x7fffffff
S_ABS_I32(0x80000000) => 0x80000000        // Note this is negative!
S_ABS_I32(0x80000001) => 0x7fffffff
S_ABS_I32(0x80000002) => 0x7ffffffe
S_ABS_I32(0xffffffff) => 0x00000001

s_bcnt0_i32_b32.description:
Count the number of "0" bits in a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is
nonzero.

s_bcnt0_i32_b64.description:
Count the number of "0" bits in a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is
nonzero.

s_bcnt1_i32_b32.description:
Count the number of "1" bits in a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is
nonzero.

s_bcnt1_i32_b64.description:
Count the number of "1" bits in a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is
nonzero.

s_quadmask_b32.description:
Reduce a pixel mask from the scalar input into a quad mask, store the result in a scalar register and set SCC iff
the result is nonzero.
s_quadmask_b32.notes: To perform the inverse operation see S_BITREPLICATE_B64_B32.

s_quadmask_b64.description:
Reduce a pixel mask from the scalar input into a quad mask, store the result in a scalar register and set SCC iff
the result is nonzero.
s_quadmask_b64.notes: To perform the inverse operation see S_BITREPLICATE_B64_B32.

s_wqm_b32.description:
Given an active pixel mask in a scalar input, calculate whole quad mode mask for that input, store the result
into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
In whole quad mode, if any pixel in a quad is active then all pixels of the quad are marked active.

s_wqm_b64.description:
Given an active pixel mask in a scalar input, calculate whole quad mode mask for that input, store the result
into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is nonzero.
In whole quad mode, if any pixel in a quad is active then all pixels of the quad are marked active.

s_not_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise negation on a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is
nonzero.

s_not_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise negation on a scalar input, store the result into a scalar register and set SCC iff the result is
nonzero.

s_and_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_and_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_or_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise OR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set
SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination
register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_or_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise OR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask, set
SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar destination
register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_xor_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise XOR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_xor_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise XOR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_nand_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise NAND on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.

s_nand_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise NAND on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.

s_nor_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise NOR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.

s_nor_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise NOR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.

s_xnor_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise XNOR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.

s_xnor_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise XNOR on the scalar input and the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the EXEC mask,
set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the scalar
destination register.

s_and_not0_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into
the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into
the scalar destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_and_not0_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into
the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into
the scalar destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_or_not0_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise OR on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into the
EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the
scalar destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_or_not0_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise OR on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into the
EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the
scalar destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_and_not1_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into
the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into
the scalar destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_and_not1_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into
the EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into
the scalar destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_or_not1_saveexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise OR on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the
EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the
scalar destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_or_not1_saveexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise OR on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into the
EXEC mask, set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero and store the original value of the EXEC mask into the
scalar destination register.
The original EXEC mask is saved to the destination SGPRs before the bitwise operation is performed.

s_and_not0_wrexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into
the EXEC mask and also into the scalar destination register, and set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero.
Unlike the SAVEEXEC series of opcodes, the value written to destination SGPRs is the result of the bitwise-op
result. EXEC and the destination SGPRs have the same value at the end of this instruction. This instruction is
intended to help accelerate waterfalling.

s_and_not0_wrexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the EXEC mask and the negation of the scalar input, store the calculated result into
the EXEC mask and also into the scalar destination register, and set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero.
Unlike the SAVEEXEC series of opcodes, the value written to destination SGPRs is the result of the bitwise-op
result. EXEC and the destination SGPRs have the same value at the end of this instruction. This instruction is
intended to help accelerate waterfalling.

s_and_not1_wrexec_b32.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into
the EXEC mask and also into the scalar destination register, and set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero.
Unlike the SAVEEXEC series of opcodes, the value written to destination SGPRs is the result of the bitwise-op
result. EXEC and the destination SGPRs have the same value at the end of this instruction. This instruction is
intended to help accelerate waterfalling.

s_and_not1_wrexec_b64.description:
Calculate bitwise AND on the scalar input and the negation of the EXEC mask, store the calculated result into
the EXEC mask and also into the scalar destination register, and set SCC iff the calculated result is nonzero.
Unlike the SAVEEXEC series of opcodes, the value written to destination SGPRs is the result of the bitwise-op
result. EXEC and the destination SGPRs have the same value at the end of this instruction. This instruction is
intended to help accelerate waterfalling.

s_movrels_b32.description: Move data from a relatively-indexed scalar register into another scalar register.
s_movrels_b32.notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move s5 <= s17:
s_mov_b32 m0, 10
s_movrels_b32 s5, s7

s_movrels_b64.description:
Move data from a relatively-indexed scalar register into another scalar register.
The index in M0.u and the operand address in SRC0.u must be even for this operation.

s_movreld_b32.description: Move data from a scalar input into a relatively-indexed scalar register.
s_movreld_b32.notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move s15 <= s7:
s_mov_b32 m0, 10
s_movreld_b32 s5, s7

s_movreld_b64.description:
Move data from a scalar input into a relatively-indexed scalar register.
The index in M0.u and the operand address in DST.u must be even for this operation.

s_movrelsd_2_b32.description:
Move data from a relatively-indexed scalar register into another relatively-indexed scalar register, using
different offsets for each index.
s_movrelsd_2_b32.notes:
Example: The following instruction sequence performs the move s25 <= s17:
s_mov_b32 m0, ((20 << 16) | 10)
s_movrelsd_2_b32 s5, s7

s_getpc_b64.description:
Store the address of the next instruction to a scalar register.
The byte address of the instruction immediately following this instruction is saved to the destination.

s_setpc_b64.description:
Jump to an address specified in a scalar register.
The argument is a byte address of the instruction to jump to.

s_swappc_b64.description:
Store the address of the next instruction to a scalar register and then jump to an address specified in the scalar
input.
The argument is a byte address of the instruction to jump to. The byte address of the instruction immediately
following this instruction is saved to the destination.

s_rfe_b64.description:
Return from the exception handler. Clear the wave's PRIV bit and then jump to an address specified by the
scalar input.
The argument is a byte address of the instruction to jump to; this address is likely derived from the state passed
into the trap handler.
This instruction may only be used within a trap handler.

s_sendmsg_rtn_b32.description:
Send a message to upstream control hardware.
SSRC[7:0] contains the message type encoded in the instruction directly (this instruction does not read an
SGPR). The message is expected to return a response from the upstream control hardware and the result is
written to SDST. Use S_WAIT_KMCNT to wait for the response on the dependent instruction.
S_SENDMSG_RTN* instructions return data in-order among themselves but out-of-order with other
instructions that manipulate lgkmcnt (including S_SENDMSG and S_SENDMSGHALT).
If the message returns a 64 bit value then only the lower 32 bits are written to SDST.
If SDST is VCC then VCCZ is undefined.

s_sendmsg_rtn_b64.description:
Send a message to upstream control hardware.
SSRC[7:0] contains the message type encoded in the instruction directly (this instruction does not read an
SGPR). The message is expected to return a response from the upstream control hardware and the result is
written to SDST. Use S_WAIT_KMCNT to wait for the response on the dependent instruction.
S_SENDMSG_RTN* instructions return data in-order among themselves but out-of-order with other
instructions that manipulate lgkmcnt (including S_SENDMSG and S_SENDMSGHALT).
If the message returns a 32 bit value then this instruction fills the upper bits of SDST with zero.
If SDST is VCC then VCCZ is undefined.

s_barrier_signal.description:
Signal that a wave has arrived at a barrier. The argument specifies which barrier to signal.
Support for M0 as an operand is reserved for other architectures.

s_barrier_signal_isfirst.description:
Signal that a wave has arrived at a barrier and set SCC to indicate if this is the first wave to signal the barrier.
The argument specifies which barrier to signal.
Support for M0 as an operand is reserved for other architectures.

s_get_barrier_state.description:
Read out current barrier state. Increments KMCNT on issue and decrements KMCNT when operation
completes.
s_get_barrier_state.notes: Use S_WAIT_KMCNT to determine when D0 can be read.

s_alloc_vgpr.description:
Attempt to set the wave's VGPR allocation to the specified number of VGPRs. The desired VGPR count may be
specified as a constant or in an SGPR. The request is rounded up to the next block size so a successful allocation
may include more than the requested number of VGPRs.
Depending on the current allocation, executing this instruction may cause additional logical VGPRs to be
allocated or it may cause logical VGPRs to be released. This operation may also return a failure code if it is
unable to allocate the requested number of VGPRs.
The 1-bit success or failure status of the request is returned to the SCC register. Success is indicated by setting
SCC to 1. A failure can occur if there are not enough free registers. The shader must check the status and is
expected to generally implement a retry loop.
VGPRs allocated by SPI cannot be fully deallocated. Only registers allocated by a prior S_ALLOC_VGPR call can
be deallocated.
s_alloc_vgpr.notes:
By default forward progress is ensured for one wave on each SIMD. Software may implement its own scheme
for ensuring forward progress if wave allocation requests exceed the available VGPRs.
Allocation is atomic --- either the full request is allocated or no registers are allocated.
The instruction buffer waits for idle both before and after changing the allocation of VGPRs, with the exception
that STORECNT may be nonzero. This allows for a reduction in VGPRs while memory writes are pending as
long as they have read all of their inputs. No following instruction can issue until the allocation operation is
complete (or allocation has failed).
MSG_DEALLOC_VGPRS is incompatible with Dynamic VGPR mode.
This instruction is illegal when in wave64 mode . This instruction is also illegal when the shader is not a CS
shader or when dynamic VGPR mode is disabled (DVGPR_EN == 0).

s_sleep_var.description:
Cause a wave to sleep for up to ~8000 clocks, or to sleep until an external event wakes the wave up.
S0[6:0] determines the sleep duration. The wave sleeps for (64*(S0[6:0]-1) … 64*S0[6:0]) clocks. The exact
amount of delay is approximate. Compare with S_NOP. When S0[6:0] is zero then no sleep occurs.
This instruction does not support "Sleep Forever" mode. To enable that mode the shader must use S_SLEEP.
See also S_SLEEP.

s_ceil_f32.description:
Round the single-precision float input up to next integer and store the result in floating point format into a
scalar register.

s_floor_f32.description:
Round the single-precision float input down to previous integer and store the result in floating point format
into a scalar register.

s_trunc_f32.description:
Compute the integer part of a single-precision float input using round toward zero semantics and store the
result in floating point format into a scalar register.

s_rndne_f32.description:
Round the single-precision float input to the nearest even integer and store the result in floating point format
into a scalar register.
s_rndne_f32.operation:
D0.f32 = floor(S0.f32 + 0.5F);
if (isEven(64'F(floor(S0.f32))) && (fract(S0.f32) == 0.5F)) then
D0.f32 -= 1.0F
endif

s_cvt_f32_i32.description:
Convert from a signed 32-bit integer input to a single-precision float value and store the result into a scalar
register.

s_cvt_f32_u32.description:
Convert from an unsigned 32-bit integer input to a single-precision float value and store the result into a scalar
register.

s_cvt_i32_f32.description:
Convert from a single-precision float input to a signed 32-bit integer value and store the result into a scalar
register.

s_cvt_u32_f32.description:
Convert from a single-precision float input to an unsigned 32-bit integer value and store the result into a scalar
register.

s_cvt_f16_f32.description:
Convert from a single-precision float input to a half-precision float value and store the result into a scalar
register.

s_cvt_f32_f16.description:
Convert from a half-precision float input to a single-precision float value and store the result into a scalar
register.

s_cvt_hi_f32_f16.description:
Convert from a half-precision float value in the high 16 bits of a scalar input to a single-precision float value
and store the result into a scalar register.

s_ceil_f16.description:
Round the half-precision float input up to next integer and store the result in floating point format into a scalar
register.

s_floor_f16.description:
Round the half-precision float input down to previous integer and store the result in floating point format into
a scalar register.

s_trunc_f16.description:
Compute the integer part of a half-precision float input using round toward zero semantics and store the result
in floating point format into a scalar register.

s_cmp_eq_i32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is equal to the second scalar input.
s_cmp_eq_i32.notes: Note that S_CMP_EQ_I32 and S_CMP_EQ_U32 are identical opcodes, but both are provided for symmetry.

s_cmp_lg_i32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or greater than the second scalar input.
s_cmp_lg_i32.notes: Note that S_CMP_LG_I32 and S_CMP_LG_U32 are identical opcodes, but both are provided for symmetry.

s_cmp_gt_i32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_ge_i32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than or equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_lt_i32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_le_i32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_eq_u32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is equal to the second scalar input.
s_cmp_eq_u32.notes: Note that S_CMP_EQ_I32 and S_CMP_EQ_U32 are identical opcodes, but both are provided for symmetry.

s_cmp_lg_u32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or greater than the second scalar input.
s_cmp_lg_u32.notes: Note that S_CMP_LG_I32 and S_CMP_LG_U32 are identical opcodes, but both are provided for symmetry.

s_cmp_gt_u32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_ge_u32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than or equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_lt_u32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_le_u32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or equal to the second scalar input.

s_bitcmp0_b32.description:
Extract a bit from the first scalar input based on an index in the second scalar input, and set SCC to 1 iff the
extracted bit is equal to 0.

s_bitcmp1_b32.description:
Extract a bit from the first scalar input based on an index in the second scalar input, and set SCC to 1 iff the
extracted bit is equal to 1.

s_bitcmp0_b64.description:
Extract a bit from the first scalar input based on an index in the second scalar input, and set SCC to 1 iff the
extracted bit is equal to 0.

s_bitcmp1_b64.description:
Extract a bit from the first scalar input based on an index in the second scalar input, and set SCC to 1 iff the
extracted bit is equal to 1.

s_cmp_eq_u64.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_lg_u64.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or greater than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_lt_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_lt_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_eq_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_eq_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_le_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_le_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_gt_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_gt_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_lg_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or greater than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_lg_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is less than or greater than the second scalar input.

s_cmp_ge_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than or equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_ge_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is greater than or equal to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_o_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is orderable to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_o_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is orderable to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_u_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not orderable to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_u_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not orderable to the second scalar input.

s_cmp_nge_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not greater than or equal to the second scalar input.
s_cmp_nge_f32.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f32 >= S1.f32);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as <

s_cmp_nge_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not greater than or equal to the second scalar input.
s_cmp_nge_f16.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f16 >= S1.f16);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as <

s_cmp_nlg_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not less than or greater than the second scalar input.
s_cmp_nlg_f32.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f32 <> S1.f32);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as ==

s_cmp_nlg_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not less than or greater than the second scalar input.
s_cmp_nlg_f16.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f16 <> S1.f16);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as ==

s_cmp_ngt_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not greater than the second scalar input.
s_cmp_ngt_f32.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f32 > S1.f32);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as <=

s_cmp_ngt_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not greater than the second scalar input.
s_cmp_ngt_f16.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f16 > S1.f16);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as <=

s_cmp_nle_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not less than or equal to the second scalar input.
s_cmp_nle_f32.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f32 <= S1.f32);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as >

s_cmp_nle_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not less than or equal to the second scalar input.
s_cmp_nle_f16.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f16 <= S1.f16);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as >

s_cmp_neq_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not equal to the second scalar input.
s_cmp_neq_f32.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f32 == S1.f32);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as !=

s_cmp_neq_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not equal to the second scalar input.
s_cmp_neq_f16.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f16 == S1.f16);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as !=

s_cmp_nlt_f32.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not less than the second scalar input.
s_cmp_nlt_f32.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f32 < S1.f32);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as >=

s_cmp_nlt_f16.description: Set SCC to 1 iff the first scalar input is not less than the second scalar input.
s_cmp_nlt_f16.operation:
SCC = !(S0.f16 < S1.f16);
// With NAN inputs this is not the same operation as >=

s_nop.description:
Do nothing. Delay issue of next instruction by a small, fixed amount.
Insert 0..15 wait states based on SIMM16[3:0]. 0x0 means the next instruction can issue on the next clock, 0xf
means the next instruction can issue 16 clocks later.
s_nop.operation:
for i in 0U : SIMM16.u16[3 : 0].u32 do
nop()
endfor
s_nop.notes:
This instruction may be used to introduce wait states to resolve hazards; see the shader programming guide for
details. Compare with S_SLEEP.
Examples:
s_nop 0           // Wait 1 cycle.
s_nop 0xf         // Wait 16 cycles.

s_setkill.description:
Kill this wave if the least significant bit of the immediate constant is 1.
Used primarily for debugging kill wave host command behavior.

s_sleep.description:
Cause a wave to sleep for up to ~8000 clocks, or to sleep until an external event wakes the wave up.
SIMM16[6:0] determines the sleep duration. The wave sleeps for (64*(SIMM16[6:0]-1) … 64*SIMM16[6:0])
clocks. The exact amount of delay is approximate. Compare with S_NOP. When SIMM16[6:0] is zero then no
sleep occurs.
"Sleep Forever" mode may also be enabled to cause a wave to sleep until an external event wakes it up.
The SIMM16 argument is encoded as follows:
DURATION = SIMM16[6:0]
s_sleep.operation:
Determines the sleep duration. The wave sleeps for (64*(SIMM16[6:0]-1) .. 64*SIMM16[6:0]) clocks. The
exact amount of delay is approximate. Compare with S_NOP. When set to zero, no sleep occurs.

SLEEP_FOREVER = SIMM16[15]
If set to 1, enables "sleep forever" mode. The wave sleeps until woken up by one of the following:

• S_WAKEUP
• S_WAKEUP_BARRIER
• An exception
• Host trap
• Other trap events
• Wave KILL
s_sleep.notes:
See also S_SLEEP_VAR.
Examples:
s_sleep { duration: 0 }        // Wait for 0 clocks.
s_sleep { duration: 1 }        // Wait for 1-64 clocks.
s_sleep { duration: 2 }        // Wait for 65-128 clocks.
s_sleep { sleep_forever: 1 }        // Wait until an event occurs.

s_clause.description:
Mark the beginning of a clause.
The next instruction determines the clause type, which may be one of the following types.
s_clause.operation:
• Image Load (non-sample instructions )






• Image Sample
• Image Store
• Image Atomic
• Buffer/Global/Scratch Load
• Buffer/Global/Scratch Store
• Buffer/Global/Scratch Atomic
• Flat Load
• Flat Store
• Flat Atomic
• LDS (loads, stores, atomics may be in same clause)
• Scalar Memory
• Vector ALU
s_clause.notes:
Once the clause type is determined, any instruction encountered within the clause that is not of the same type
(and not an internal instruction described below) is illegal and may lead to undefined behaviour. Attempting to
issue S_CLAUSE while inside a clause is also illegal.
Instructions that are processed internally do not interrupt the clause. The following instructions are internal:
• S_NOP,
• S_WAIT_*CNT,
• S_DELAY_ALU.
Halting or killing a wave breaks the clause. VALU exceptions and other traps that cause the shader to enter its
trap handler breaks the clause. The single-step debug mode breaks the clause.
The clause length must be between 2 and 63 instructions, inclusive. Clause breaks may be from 1 to 15, or may
be disabled entirely. Clause length and breaks are encoded in the SIMM16 argument as follows:
LENGTH = SIMM16[5:0]
This field is set to the logical number of instructions in the clause, minus 1 (e.g. if a clause has 4
instructions, program this field to 3). The minimum number of instructions required for a clause is 2 and
the maximum number of instructions is 63, therefore this field must be programmed in the range [1, 62]
inclusive.
BREAK_SPAN = SIMM16[11:8]
This field is set to the number of instructions to issue before each clause break. If set to zero then there are
no clause breaks. If set to nonzero value then the maximum number of instructions between clause breaks
is 15.
The following instruction types cannot appear in a clause:
• Instructions of a different type than the clause type, as determined by the first instruction in the clause
• S_CLAUSE
• S_ENDPGM
• SALU
• Branch
• Message
• S_SLEEP
• S_SLEEP_VAR
• VDSDIR
• VINTERP
• Export
• S_SETHALT
• S_SETKILL
To schedule an S_WAIT_* or S_DELAY_ALU instruction for the first instruction in the clause, the waitcnt/delay
instruction must appear before the S_CLAUSE instruction so that S_CLAUSE can accurately determine the
clause type.
S_DELAY_ALU is orthogonal to S_CLAUSE; ALU clauses should be structured to avoid any stalling.

s_delay_alu.description:
Insert delay between dependent SALU/VALU instructions.
The SIMM16 argument is encoded as:
INSTID0 = SIMM16[3:0]
s_delay_alu.operation:
Hazard to delay for with the next VALU instruction.

INSTSKIP = SIMM16[6:4]
Identify the VALU instruction that the second delay condition applies to.

INSTID1 = SIMM16[10:7]
Hazard to delay for with the VALU instruction identified by INSTSKIP.
s_delay_alu.notes:
Legal values for the InstID0 and InstID1 fields are:
INSTID_NO_DEP (0x0)
No dependency on any prior instruction.
INSTID_VALU_DEP_1 (0x1)
Dependent on previous VALU instruction, 1 instruction(s) back.
INSTID_VALU_DEP_2 (0x2)
Dependent on previous VALU instruction, 2 instruction(s) back.
INSTID_VALU_DEP_3 (0x3)
Dependent on previous VALU instruction, 3 instruction(s) back.
INSTID_VALU_DEP_4 (0x4)
Dependent on previous VALU instruction, 4 instruction(s) back.
INSTID_TRANS32_DEP_1 (0x5)
Dependent on previous TRANS32 instruction, 1 instruction(s) back.
INSTID_TRANS32_DEP_2 (0x6)
Dependent on previous TRANS32 instruction, 2 instruction(s) back.
INSTID_TRANS32_DEP_3 (0x7)
Dependent on previous TRANS32 instruction, 3 instruction(s) back.
INSTID_FMA_ACCUM_CYCLE_1 (0x8)
Single cycle penalty for FMA accumulation (reserved).
INSTID_SALU_CYCLE_1 (0x9)
1 cycle penalty for a prior SALU instruction.
INSTID_SALU_CYCLE_2 (0xa)
2 cycle penalty for a prior SALU instruction.
INSTID_SALU_CYCLE_3 (0xb)
3 cycle penalty for a prior SALU instruction.
Legal values for the InstSkip field are:
INSTSKIP_SAME (0x0)
Apply second dependency to same instruction (2 dependencies on one instruction).
INSTSKIP_NEXT (0x1)
Apply second dependency to next instruction (no skip).
INSTSKIP_SKIP_1 (0x2)
Skip 1 instruction(s) then apply dependency.
INSTSKIP_SKIP_2 (0x3)
Skip 2 instruction(s) then apply dependency.
INSTSKIP_SKIP_3 (0x4)
Skip 3 instruction(s) then apply dependency.
INSTSKIP_SKIP_4 (0x5)
Skip 4 instruction(s) then apply dependency.
This instruction describes dependencies for two instructions, directing the hardware to insert delay if the
dependent instruction was issued too recently to forward data to the second.
S_DELAY_ALU instructions record the required delay with respect to a previous VALU instruction and indicate
data dependencies that benefit from having extra idle cycles inserted between them. These instructions are
optional: without them the program still functions correctly but performance may suffer when multiple waves
are in flight; IB may issue dependent instructions that stall in the ALU, preventing those cycles from being
utilized by other wavefronts.
If enough independent instructions are between dependent ones then no delay is necessary and this
instruction may be omitted. For wave64 the compiler may not know the status of the EXEC mask and hence
does not know if instructions require 1 or 2 passes to issue. S_DELAY_ALU encodes the type of dependency so
that hardware may apply the correct delay depending on the number of active passes.
S_DELAY_ALU may execute in zero cycles.
To reduce instruction stream overhead the S_DELAY_ALU instructions packs two delay values into one
instruction, with a "skip" indicator so the two delayed instructions don't need to be back-to-back.
S_DELAY_ALU is illegal inside of a clause created by S_CLAUSE.
Example:
v_mov_b32 v3, v0
v_lshlrev_b32     v30, 1, v31
v_lshlrev_b32     v24, 1, v25
s_delay_alu { instid0: INSTID_VALU_DEP_3, instskip: INSTSKIP_SKIP_1, instid1: INSTID_VALU_DEP_1 }
// 1 cycle delay here
v_add_f32   v0, v1, v3
v_sub_f32   v11, v9, v9
// 2 cycles delay here
v_mul_f32   v10, v13, v11

s_waitcnt.description:
Equivalent to S_WAIT_IDLE. This opcode should not be used in modern code; use one of the specialized
S_WAIT_* instructions instead. The operand is ignored for compatibility.

s_wait_idle.description: Wait for all activity in the wave to be complete (all dependency and memory counters at zero).

s_wait_event.description:
Wait for an event to occur or a condition to be satisfied before continuing. The SIMM16 argument specifies
which event(s) to wait on.
EXPORT_READY = SIMM16[1]
s_wait_event.operation:
If this value is ONE then sleep until the export_ready bit is 1. If the export_ready bit is already 1, no sleep
occurs. Effect is the same as the export_ready check performed before issuing an export instruction.
s_wait_event.notes:
No wait occurs if this value is ZERO.
This wait cannot be preempted by KILL, context-save, host trap, single-step or trap after instruction events. IB
waits for the event to occur before processing internal or external exceptions which can delay entry to the trap
handler for a significant amount of time.

s_trap.description:
Enter the trap handler.
This instruction may be generated internally as well in response to a host trap (TrapID = 0) or an exception.
TrapID 0 is reserved for hardware use and should not be used in a shader-generated trap.

s_round_mode.description:
Set floating point round mode using an immediate constant.
Avoids wait state penalty that would be imposed by S_SETREG.

s_denorm_mode.description:
Set floating point denormal mode using an immediate constant.
Avoids wait state penalty that would be imposed by S_SETREG.

s_barrier_wait.description: Wait for a barrier to complete. The SIMM16 argument specifies which barrier to wait on.

s_code_end.description:
Generate an illegal instruction interrupt. This instruction is used to mark the end of a shader buffer for debug
tools.
This instruction should not appear in typical shader code. It is used to pad the end of a shader program to make
it easier for analysis programs to locate the end of a shader program buffer. Use of this opcode in an embedded
shader block may cause analysis tools to fail.
To unambiguously mark the end of a shader buffer, this instruction must be specified five times in a row (total
of 20 bytes) and analysis tools must ensure the opcode occurs at least five times to be certain they are at the end
of the buffer. This is because the bit pattern generated by this opcode could incidentally appear in a valid
instruction's second dword, literal constant or as part of a multi-DWORD image instruction.
In short: do not embed this opcode in the middle of a valid shader program. DO use this opcode 5 times at the
end of a shader program to clearly mark the end of the program.
Example:
s_code_end.operation:
...
s_endpgm       // last real instruction in shader buffer
s_code_end        // 1
s_code_end        // 2
s_code_end        // 3
s_code_end        // 4
s_code_end        // done!

s_branch.description:
Jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC.
The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
s_branch.notes:
For a long jump or an indirect jump use S_SETPC_B64.
Examples:
s_branch label      // Set SIMM16 = +4 = 0x0004
s_nop 0      // 4 bytes
label:
s_nop 0      // 4 bytes
s_branch label      // Set SIMM16 = -8 = 0xfff8

s_cbranch_scc0.description:
If SCC is 0 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC.
The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
s_cbranch_scc0.operation:
if SCC == 1'0U then
PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
else
PC = PC + 4LL
endif

s_cbranch_scc1.description:
If SCC is 1 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC.
The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
s_cbranch_scc1.operation:
if SCC == 1'1U then
PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
else
PC = PC + 4LL
endif

s_cbranch_vccz.description:
If VCCZ is 1 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC.
The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
s_cbranch_vccz.operation:
if VCCZ.u1 == 1'1U then
PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
else
PC = PC + 4LL
endif

s_cbranch_vccnz.description:
If VCCZ is 0 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC.
The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
s_cbranch_vccnz.operation:
if VCCZ.u1 == 1'0U then







PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
else
PC = PC + 4LL
endif

s_cbranch_execz.description:
If EXECZ is 1 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC.
The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
s_cbranch_execz.operation:
if EXECZ.u1 == 1'1U then
PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
else
PC = PC + 4LL
endif

s_cbranch_execnz.description:
If EXECZ is 0 then jump to a constant offset relative to the current PC.
The literal argument is a signed DWORD offset relative to the PC of the next instruction.
s_cbranch_execnz.operation:
if EXECZ.u1 == 1'0U then
PC = PC + signext(SIMM16.i16 * 16'4) + 4LL
else
PC = PC + 4LL
endif

s_endpgm.description:
End of program; terminate wavefront.
The hardware implicitly executes S_WAIT_IDLE before executing this instruction. See S_ENDPGM_SAVED for
the context-switch version of this instruction.

s_endpgm_saved.description:
End of program; signal that a wave has been saved by the context-switch trap handler and terminate
wavefront.
The hardware implicitly executes S_WAIT_IDLE before executing this instruction. See S_ENDPGM for
additional variants.

s_wakeup.description:
Allow a wave to 'ping' all the other waves in its threadgroup to force them to wake up early from an S_SLEEP
instruction.
The ping is ignored if the waves are not sleeping. This allows for efficient polling on a memory location. The
waves which are polling can sit in a long S_SLEEP between memory reads, but the wave which writes the value
can tell them all to wake up early now that the data is available. This method is also safe from races since any
waves that miss the ping resume when they complete their S_SLEEP.
If the wave executing S_WAKEUP is in a threadgroup (in_wg set), then it wakes up all waves associated with the
same threadgroup ID. Otherwise, S_WAKEUP is treated as an S_NOP.

s_setprio.description:
Change wave user priority.
User settable wave priority is set to SIMM16[1:0]. 0 is the lowest priority and 3 is the highest. The overall wave
priority is:
s_setprio.operation: SysUserPrio = MIN(3, SysPrio[1:0] + UserPrio[1:0]). Priority = {SysUserPrio[1:0], WaveAge[3:0]}
s_setprio.notes: The system priority cannot be modified from within the wave.

s_sendmsg.description:
Send a message to upstream control hardware.
SIMM16[7:0] contains the message type.
s_sendmsg.notes: Message types are documented in the shader programming guide.

s_sendmsghalt.description: Send a message to upstream control hardware and then HALT the wavefront; see S_SENDMSG for details.

s_incperflevel.description: Increment performance counter specified in SIMM16[3:0] by 1.

s_decperflevel.description: Decrement performance counter specified in SIMM16[3:0] by 1.

s_icache_inv.description: Invalidate entire first level instruction cache.

s_wait_loadcnt.description: Wait until LOADCNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[5:0].

s_wait_storecnt.description: Wait until STORECNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[5:0].

s_wait_samplecnt.description: Wait until SAMPLECNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[5:0].

s_wait_expcnt.description: Wait until EXPCNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[2:0].

s_wait_dscnt.description: Wait until DSCNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[5:0].

s_wait_kmcnt.description: Wait until KMCNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[4:0].

s_wait_loadcnt_dscnt.description:
Wait until LOADCNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[13:8] and DSCNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[5:0].
Argument is a bitfield of which dependency counters to wait to be zero. The SIMM16 argument is encoded as:
DS = SIMM16[5:0]
s_wait_loadcnt_dscnt.operation:
Wait for DSCNT <= N.

MEM = SIMM16[13:8]
Wait for either LOADCNT <= N or STORECNT <= N (depending on instruction).

s_wait_storecnt_dscnt.description:
Wait until STORECNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[13:8] and DSCNT is less than or equal to SIMM16[5:0].
Argument is a bitfield of which dependency counters to wait to be zero. The SIMM16 argument is encoded as:
DS = SIMM16[5:0]
s_wait_storecnt_dscnt.operation:
Wait for DSCNT <= N.

MEM = SIMM16[13:8]
Wait for either LOADCNT <= N or STORECNT <= N (depending on instruction).

s_load_b32.description: Load 32 bits of data from the scalar memory into a scalar register.
s_load_b32.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_b64.description: Load 64 bits of data from the scalar memory into a scalar register.
s_load_b64.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_b128.description: Load 128 bits of data from the scalar memory into a scalar register.
s_load_b128.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_b256.description: Load 256 bits of data from the scalar memory into a scalar register.
s_load_b256.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_b512.description: Load 512 bits of data from the scalar memory into a scalar register.
s_load_b512.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_b96.description: Load 96 bits of data from the scalar memory into a scalar register.
s_load_b96.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_i8.description:
Load 8 bits of signed data from the scalar memory, sign extend to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar
register.
s_load_i8.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_u8.description:
Load 8 bits of unsigned data from the scalar memory, zero extend to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar
register.
s_load_u8.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_i16.description:
Load 16 bits of signed data from the scalar memory, sign extend to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar
register.
s_load_i16.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_load_u16.description:
Load 16 bits of unsigned data from the scalar memory, zero extend to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar
register.
s_load_u16.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_buffer_load_b32.description: Load 32 bits of data from a scalar buffer surface into a scalar register.
s_buffer_load_b32.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_buffer_load_b64.description: Load 64 bits of data from a scalar buffer surface into a scalar register.
s_buffer_load_b64.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_buffer_load_b128.description: Load 128 bits of data from a scalar buffer surface into a scalar register.
s_buffer_load_b128.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_buffer_load_b256.description: Load 256 bits of data from a scalar buffer surface into a scalar register.

If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_buffer_load_b512.description: Load 512 bits of data from a scalar buffer surface into a scalar register.
s_buffer_load_b512.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_buffer_load_b96.description: Load 96 bits of data from a scalar buffer surface into a scalar register.
s_buffer_load_b96.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_buffer_load_i8.description:
Load 8 bits of signed data from a scalar buffer surface, sign extend to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar
register.
s_buffer_load_i8.notes:
If the offset is specified as an SGPR, the SGPR contains an UNSIGNED BYTE offset (the 2 LSBs are ignored).
If the offset is specified as an immediate 21-bit constant, the constant is a SIGNED BYTE offset.

s_buffer_load_u8.description:
Load 8 bits of unsigned data from a scalar buffer surface, zero extend to 32 bits and store the result into a scalar
